channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fires break out in Broadbeach and Clontarf, one person feared dead
item.description: On Saturday, May 30, 2026, two significant fires erupted near Brisbane, causing major concern for residents and emergency services. The first blaze occurred at the Neptune resort on Surf Parade in Bro...

item.title: Exploring BRGV-OS: A Lightweight and Developer-Friendly Linux Distribution
item.description: Jairo Junior shares his experience switching from Fedora to BRGV-OS, a Linux distribution built on Void Linux. Initially unfamiliar with Void Linux, Jairo was drawn to BRGV-OS for its emphasis on gami...

item.title: Dis-Chem reports lower earnings and cuts dividend amid strategic expansion
item.description: Dis-Chem Pharmacies saw its share price drop by 8.9% on the JSE on Friday, closing at R34.55, following the announcement of a decline in both earnings per share (EPS) and dividends. While group revenu...

item.title: Pakistan tops Erasmus Mundus scholarship rankings globally for fifth straight year with 98 awards
item.description: Pakistan has once again achieved a remarkable milestone in international education by securing the highest number of Erasmus Mundus scholarships globally for the fifth consecutive year. According to t...

item.title: Effective treatments to rejuvenate and firm the neck: Expert insights
item.description: As we age, the neck often shows signs of sagging, crepey skin, and loss of definition, which many find bothersome. Beauty expert Alice Hart-Davis shares her extensive personal experience with various ...

item.title: Equity Bank announces multiple job vacancies with set application deadlines for Kenyan professionals
item.description: Equity Bank has announced a fresh round of job opportunities targeting Kenyan professionals across several departments, with strict application deadlines for interested candidates. The bank, through i...

item.title: Ogie Alcasid playfully enclosed in a box by Karylle on It’s Showtime segment
item.description: In a fun segment on It’s Showtime, veteran singer and host Ogie Alcasid was playfully placed inside a large box by fellow host Karylle, creating a moment of laughter and surprise for the live audience...

item.title: Dell Reports Record Quarterly Revenue Driven by AI Server Sales
item.description: Dell Technologies experienced an unprecedented surge in its stock price, climbing 32.76% in a single day following the announcement of its first-quarter earnings. This performance marks the company's ...

item.title: Ben Pol reflects on rise, financial struggles and music comeback
item.description: Tanzanian singer-songwriter Ben Pol has opened up about his personal and professional journey, sharing how he rose to fame at a young age, later faced serious financial hardship, and eventually manage...

item.title: Qudus Ibrahim discusses QIB Productions and the rise of interactive theatre in Nigeria
item.description: Qudus Ibrahim is a Nigerian creative entrepreneur and the founder and CEO of QIB Productions, a company widely recognised for pioneering interactive and immersive theatre in Nigeria. Through his work,...

item.title: Marcia Lucas, Pioneering 'Star Wars' Editor, Dies at 80
item.description: Marcia Lucas, the acclaimed film editor known for her work on the original 'Star Wars' and its sequels, has passed away at the age of 80 due to metastatic cancer. A key figure in the New Hollywood era...

item.title: Reconstructing a Father Through Memories and Stories
item.description: Akinola Davies Jr. lose him papa wen e be only twenty months old, while him brother Wale dey four. Now, dem two bros don come together create one film wey dey try build the picture of their papa, man ...

item.title: Man seriously injured after car mounts footpath in North Beach crash
item.description: A man in his 60s has suffered serious injuries after being struck by a vehicle that mounted a footpath in North Beach, Western Australia, on Saturday morning. The incident occurred just after 11am on ...

item.title: How 19th-Century Whaling Routes Help Explain a Rare Providence–Cape Verde Flight
item.description: The article explains the surprising existence of a direct transatlantic flight between Providence, Rhode Island, and Cape Verde, one of the most unusual international routes in North America. Providen...

item.title: Donald Trump declared fit for duty after comprehensive medical examination at Walter Reed
item.description: US President Donald Trump has been declared in excellent health following an extensive medical examination conducted at Walter Reed National Military Medical Centre. The assessment involved 22 special...

item.title: Next week preview: jobs data, Broadcom and Target earnings cues for markets
item.description: The next week is shaping up as a critical period for investors as the markets await key data and earnings reports. Broadcom is among the prominent semiconductor names delivering quarterly results, wit...

item.title: Kenya National Highways Authority Orders Traders to Vacate Kamulu-Joska Road for Road Upgrades
item.description: The Kenya National Highways Authority (KeNHA) has issued a 30-day eviction notice to all traders and unauthorised structures along the Kamulu-Joska-Malaa-Tala-Kangundo (B63) Road. The notice, released...

item.title: Mwananchi Journalists Receive Multiple Honours at Tanzania's Ejat Awards
item.description: Journalists from Mwananchi newspaper have earned significant recognition at the Ejat Awards, one of Tanzania’s key journalism awards that celebrate excellence in media practice. Reporters and editors ...

item.title: Judi Singh: The overlooked Indo-Canadian jazz singer and her enduring musical legacy
item.description: Judi Singh, also spelled Judy Singh, was a highly talented Indo-Canadian jazz vocalist whose career flourished in the 1960s and 1970s but never received the mainstream recognition it deserved. Born in...

item.title: South Africa must act to curb rising nicotine addiction among youth
item.description: As South Africa marks World No Tobacco Day, the issue of rising nicotine addiction among young people is becoming increasingly urgent. Research from the University of Pretoria shows a significant rise...
